titleStmt.title: SII 17.216: original edition by K. G. Krishnan
No. 216. (A.R. No. 194 of 1904.) TIRUVAKKARAI, VILUPPURAM TALUK, SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT.

summary.p: On the south base of the 1,000 pillared maṇḍapa inside the second prākāra of the Chandramauḷīśvara temple.

On palæographical grounds and on account of the donor Ammai Appaṉ Pāṇḍināḍukōṇḍāṉ Gaṇḍar Sūriyaṉ Śambuvarāyaṉ who is known to have been a feudatory of both Rājādhirāja II and Kulōttuṅga III (vide No. 212 above and No. 217 below), this record may be assigned to Kulōttuṅga III.
This records the gift of two five-faceted lamps with stands including iron chains to god Āḷuḍaiyār of Tiruvakkarai by Ammai Appaṉ Pāṇḍi-nāḍu-koṇḍāṉ Gaṇḍar Sūriyaṉ Śambuvarāyaṉ.

revisionDesc.change: Further conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)
Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
svastiśrī . tiripuvaṉaccakkaravarttikaḷ kulottuṅkacoḻadevaṟku yāṇ
ṭu 10 6 vatu Uṭaiyār tiruvakkarai Āḷuṭaiyārkku Ammai Appaṉ pā
ṇṭi nāṭu koṇṭāṉāṉa kaṇṭar cūriyaṉ campuvarāyaṉ ceyvittu Iṭṭa
Aiñcu nilaikkutti viḷakku 2 ṉāl Iruppu nārācam Uṭpaṭa tarā Eṭai Āyirattu
nūṟṟu Eṇpattu Irupalam .
Digital edition of SII 17.216 by converted to DHARMA conventions by Emmanuel Francis.
